items.title: Polynomial regression with runaway coefficients
items.scenario: A student fits degree-15 polynomial to 30 noisy points. Unregularized weights explode and validation MSE is 10× training MSE. Adding L2 weight penalty with tuned λ shrinks coefficients; validation MSE drops toward training MSE.
items.explanation: Large weights let high-degree terms chase noise. L2 shrinkage limits that flexibility—classic variance reduction via regularization.

items.title: Text classification with thousands of word features
items.scenario: A spam filter uses 50,000 word indicators but only 500 labeled emails. Unregularized logistic regression overfits rare word co-occurrences. L1 regularization zeros most word weights, keeping a sparse list of discriminative terms.
items.explanation: L1 sparsity selects a compact feature set when p ≫ n—regularization as both overfitting control and implicit feature selection.

items.title: Neural net weight decay on image data
items.scenario: A small CNN on 2,000 images reaches 99% training accuracy but 72% validation accuracy. Enabling L2 weight decay (λ = 1e-4) in the optimizer reduces the train–val gap to roughly 95% vs 88%.
items.explanation: Weight decay is L2 regularization on network weights—the same penalty concept applied beyond linear models.
